overview.problem: Design a customizable multi-provider AI usage observability dashboard with project attribution, cost/token analytics, and Claude memory-file insights
overview.approach: Build a local-first observability stack around canonical usage_event ingestion, deterministic project attribution with confidence, dual-layer cost analytics, privacy-safe Claude memory insights, and declarative customizable dashboards with optional redacted team sync.

decisions.decision: Normalize all telemetry into a canonical usage_event ledger
decisions.reasoning: cusage and codex-lb expose different token fields -> per-widget transforms drift over time -> canonical event normalization at ingest preserves consistency and replayability

decisions.decision: Use deterministic project attribution ladder with confidence scoring
decisions.reasoning: many events miss explicit project_id -> project split is mandatory -> apply ordered attribution signals and persist confidence to keep coverage auditable

decisions.decision: Track estimated and billed cost as separate metrics
decisions.reasoning: price tables are available before invoices -> invoice timing lags and can differ -> dual cost layer avoids false precision while preserving early visibility

decisions.decision: Attach freshness and attribution quality metadata to every widget query
decisions.reasoning: stale sources and weak attribution look accurate in charts -> hidden uncertainty erodes trust -> expose freshness windows and confidence bands in API responses

decisions.decision: Model dashboards as saved declarative views over a certified widget catalog
decisions.reasoning: full customization without guardrails causes metric drift -> users still need flexible layout and filters -> declarative saved views plus certified metrics balances flexibility and governance

decisions.decision: Keep storage local-first with optional redacted team sync
decisions.reasoning: usage logs and memory artifacts are sensitive by default -> teams still need sharing in some contexts -> local primary store with opt-in redacted sync satisfies both

decisions.decision: Ship ingestion as batch-first with optional tailing instead of stream-first core
decisions.reasoning: stream-first architecture raises operational burden and failure modes -> v1 needs dependable local operation -> batch snapshots plus optional tails gives lower risk path

decisions.decision: Define a framework-agnostic view and widget API contract before frontend framework commitment
decisions.reasoning: framework choice remains open by requirement -> customization model must still be concrete -> stable API contract decouples product semantics from UI framework selection

decisions.decision: Analyze Claude memory files at metadata and churn levels by default
decisions.reasoning: memory content can include sensitive user context -> observability requires usage and evolution signals not raw text -> metadata-first memory analytics preserves privacy and utility

rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: Higher operational burden and retry complexity for v1 local-first deployment.
Product requirement needs Claude and OpenAI split and comparison.

constraints: Support provider split and per-project attribution for Claude and OpenAI.
Include Claude memory-file analytics in dashboard features.
Support fully customizable dashboard layouts and widgets.
Preserve local-first privacy-first operation with optional team sync.
Expose freshness and attribution confidence in metric payloads.

known_risks.risk: Project attribution false positives from weak path heuristics.
known_risks.mitigation: Store attribution reason code and confidence and allow unknown fallback instead of forced assignment.

known_risks.risk: Estimated costs diverge from billed invoices and confuse dashboard users.
known_risks.mitigation: Publish estimated and billed layers separately with explicit variance and freshness metadata.

known_risks.risk: Custom widget freedom creates metric definition drift between teams.
known_risks.mitigation: Restrict core metrics to certified widget catalog and record lineage for each widget payload.

invisible_knowledge.system: Telemetry normalization and attribution run before analytics so every widget consumes the same canonical event ledger. Confidence and freshness are first-class dimensions and must flow through APIs and UI.
invariants: Every stored usage event has provider source and attribution confidence metadata.
Metric responses carry freshness state and source timestamp.
Memory insights expose metadata and churn metrics without raw memory text.
Certified widget definitions remain stable when user layouts change.
tradeoffs: Batch-first ingestion lowers operational risk but near-real-time views can lag source writes.
Confidence-based attribution increases transparency but leaves some events in unknown project bucket.
Local-first storage improves privacy but requires optional sync service for team visibility.

milestones.name: Unified Event Ingestion and Project Attribution
requirements: Ingest Claude local usage signals and OpenAI Codex local usage signals
Map raw token fields into canonical usage_event schema
Resolve provider and project attribution using deterministic ladder with confidence score
Persist append-only usage events with source provenance and ingest timestamp
acceptance_criteria: Daily and hourly aggregates match source totals within tolerance
Every stored event includes provider and project_id or unknown with confidence field
Attribution pipeline emits explainable reason code for each event
tests: Integration test replays mixed source fixtures and verifies canonical event totals
Property style test checks attribution ladder determinism for repeated inputs
Failure test validates parser resilience for malformed JSONL lines

code_intents.behavior: Transform provider-specific token payloads into canonical usage_event records with standardized token categories, cost placeholders, source metadata, and ingest timestamp.
Apply deterministic attribution ladder across explicit project markers, session metadata, path heuristics, and unknown fallback while returning confidence and reason code.
Persist normalized events as append-only records keyed by source event id with lineage fields to support replay and audit queries.

milestones.name: Cost Token and Freshness Analytics Engine
requirements: Compute provider split token and cost aggregates at project and workspace levels
Expose estimated and billed cost as separate metrics with variance field
Attach freshness timestamp and stale classification for each metric payload
acceptance_criteria: Metrics API returns provider and project splits for all supported time buckets
Cost responses include estimated and billed layers with clear labels
Freshness metadata changes when source snapshots age past configured threshold
tests: Integration test validates metrics API against seeded canonical events
Contract test asserts freshness and confidence fields are always present
Edge test covers missing billed cost fallback behavior

code_intents.behavior: Compute estimated and billed cost layers per provider and project and include explicit variance and unknown-billing flags for downstream dashboards.
Derive freshness state from ingest lag, source scan completion, and attribution completeness and publish normalized freshness envelope for every metric.
Serve project and provider split metrics with cost layers and freshness metadata through stable API contracts used by widgets.

milestones.name: Claude Memory File Insight Pipeline
requirements: Scan Claude memory files from local workspace boundaries
Extract metadata level signals for size churn write frequency and topic tags
Publish project-level memory insight metrics without exposing raw memory text
acceptance_criteria: Memory insights API returns churn and growth trends by project
Raw memory content is absent from response contracts and persistence layer
Scanner marks unavailable files with explicit freshness and error metadata
tests: Integration test runs scanner on synthetic memory files and validates metrics
Security test asserts no raw memory body is serialized into API payload
Error test verifies missing file handling and stale markers

code_intents.behavior: Collect Claude memory file metadata including file size mtime and record counts within configured workspace boundaries without loading raw body into analytics storage.
Compute project-level churn indicators such as growth rate update cadence and retention windows from metadata snapshots.
Expose memory insight endpoints that include trend data freshness metadata and privacy-safe diagnostic fields.

milestones.name: Declarative Custom Views and Widget Query Contracts
requirements: Represent dashboards as declarative saved views with layout filter and widget bindings
Provide certified widget catalog with parameter schema and capability tags
Resolve widget queries against canonical analytics APIs with policy checks
acceptance_criteria: Users can create update and clone views without mutating certified metric definitions
Widget resolver enforces parameter validation and role-based capability constraints
Shared view payloads remain stable across backend restarts
tests: Integration test validates create update clone and delete view flows
Contract test verifies widget schema validation failures return deterministic errors
Regression test ensures saved view JSON round-trips without field loss

code_intents.behavior: Define declarative view schema covering layout, widget bindings, filter scopes, and ownership metadata with strict validation rules.
Provide certified widget registry with metric lineage, parameter schema, capability tags, and deprecation metadata.
Resolve widget requests into canonical analytics queries while enforcing parameter checks and policy constraints before execution.

milestones.name: Dashboard Workspace UX and Optional Team Sync
requirements: Render drag-and-drop dashboard workspace from declarative view contracts
Surface freshness and attribution confidence indicators within each widget shell
Allow optional team sync for saved views through redacted payload policy
acceptance_criteria: Workspace supports add move resize hide and preset actions for widgets
Widgets display freshness age and attribution confidence without custom code per widget
Team sync exports omit local sensitive identifiers unless explicit allowlist permits
tests: End-to-end test covers layout editing persistence and reload behavior
Integration test validates redaction policy before sync publication
Accessibility test checks keyboard navigation for widget layout controls

code_intents.behavior: Implement drag and resize dashboard editor that applies declarative layout operations and persists ordering and geometry changes predictably.
Render certified widget payloads with embedded freshness age and attribution confidence states and consistent uncertainty visuals.
Apply allowlist-based redaction for shared view payloads to keep sensitive local identifiers out of optional team sync artifacts.
